articles.title: This man with ALS is “the first power user” of a brain implant that lets him speak
articles.description: Casey Harrell has had a set of electrodes embedded in his brain for almost three years. Harrell, who has amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) and is paralyzed, first used his brain-computer interface (BCI) to “speak” sentences with the help of a research team in 2023. articles.title: These new solid-state ACs promise a cool future. Scientists aren’t so sure.
articles.description: After three years of record-­breaking heat, this one is set to be yet another scorcher. Air-conditioning? Not going anywhere. articles.title: Data Centers’ Next Hurdle: Winning Public Trust and Social License
articles.description: The industry faces a “public legitimacy challenge,” where earning social license and trust has become as critical to success as obtaining megawatts and permits.
